message:
fix: gt dog done auto-terminates tmux session to prevent idle-at-prompt (gt-2yx7)

Dogs completed work and sent DOG_DONE but idled at the Claude prompt
instead of exiting. The stale-working detector took 2 hours to clean
them up, causing the deacon to repeatedly kill stuck sessions.

Now `gt dog done` spawns a background process that kills the tmux
session after a 3-second delay (enough for the agent to see output).
Also updated session instructions, dog template, and plugin dispatch
mail to reflect the auto-termination behavior and remove the old
contradictory "then exit the session" instructions that dogs ignored.

files.patch:
@@ -4,8 +4,10 @@ import (
 	"encoding/json"
 	"fmt"
 	"os"
+	"os/exec"
 	"path/filepath"
 	"strings"
+	"syscall"
 	"time"
 
 	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
@@ -678,6 +680,22 @@ func runDogDone(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
 	}
 
 	fmt.Printf("✓ Dog %s returned to kennel (idle)\n", name)
+
+	// Auto-terminate the tmux session after a short delay.
+	// Dogs run inside tmux sessions (hq-dog-<name>). Without this, the
+	// Claude agent idles at the prompt indefinitely after completing work,
+	// wasting resources until the stale-working detector kills it (2 hours).
+	// The delay lets the agent see the success output before termination.
+	sessionID := fmt.Sprintf("hq-dog-%s", name)
+	fmt.Printf("  Session %s will terminate in 3s\n", sessionID)
+	killCmd := exec.Command("bash", "-c",
+		fmt.Sprintf("sleep 3 && tmux kill-session -t '%s' 2>/dev/null", sessionID))
+	killCmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}
+	if err := killCmd.Start(); err != nil {
+		// Non-fatal: session may not be tmux-based (e.g., manual testing).
+		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "warning: failed to schedule session termination: %v\n", err)
+	}
+
 	return nil
 }
 
@@ -1135,8 +1153,7 @@ func formatPluginMailBody(p *plugin.Plugin) string {
 	sb.WriteString("\n\n---\n\n")
 	sb.WriteString("After completion:\n")
 	sb.WriteString("1. Create a wisp to record the result (success/failure)\n")
-	sb.WriteString("2. Send DOG_DONE mail to deacon/\n")
-	sb.WriteString("3. Run `gt dog done`, then exit the session (do NOT idle at the prompt)\n")
+	sb.WriteString("2. Run `gt dog done` — this clears your work and auto-terminates the session\n")
 
 	return sb.String()
 }

@@ -55,12 +55,14 @@ This command:
 1. Clears your work assignment
 2. Sets your state back to "idle"
 3. Makes you available for the next dispatch
+4. **Auto-terminates your tmux session** (after a 3-second delay)
 
 **Without `{{ cmd }} dog done`**, you will be stuck in "working" state forever, and the
 Deacon cannot assign you new work.
 
 The command auto-detects your dog name from your working directory, so just run
-`{{ cmd }} dog done` with no arguments.
+`{{ cmd }} dog done` with no arguments. Your session ends automatically — do NOT
+idle at the prompt after running this command.
 
 ## Prefix-Based Routing
 
@@ -105,7 +107,7 @@ Routes defined in `{{ .TownRoot }}/.beads/routes.jsonl`. Debug with: `BD_DEBUG_R
 
 ```
 [ ] Complete all formula steps
-[ ] gt dog done              (CRITICAL - return to idle)
+[ ] gt dog done              (CRITICAL - clears work + auto-terminates session)
 ```
 
 ---
